# bitHumanKit — Full Reference for AI Agents

This is the deep-context bundle for AI coding agents. The compact
version is `llms.txt`. Read this when:
  - You're being asked to implement a non-trivial integration.
  - You need to reason about an error or expand on existing patterns.

Repo: https://github.com/bithuman-product/bithuman-kit
Made by: https://www.bithuman.ai

================================================================
SECTION 1 — REPO LAYOUT
================================================================

```
bithuman-kit/
├── Package.swift                       SPM manifest
├── Sources/
│   ├── bitHumanKit/                    The library (`import bitHumanKit`)
│   ├── BithumanCLI/                    bithuman-cli executable (macOS-only)
│   ├── BithumanMac/                    BithumanMac executable target
│   ├── BithumanPad/                    bithuman-pad executable target
│   └── BithumanPhone/                  bithuman-phone executable target
├── Tests/
│   └── bitHumanKitTests/               Unit tests
├── Apps/                               Example app sources (Mac/iPad/iPhone)
├── scripts/                            Maintainer tooling
├── Examples/HelloVoiceChat/            Minimal SPM consumer example
└── Formula/bithuman-cli.rb             Homebrew formula (synced to public tap)
```

================================================================
SECTION 2 — PUBLIC API SURFACE (canonical)
================================================================

## VoiceChat

The top-level entry point. Owns one live conversation: audio graph,
speech analyzer, language model, voice synthesis, optional avatar.
Construct, call `start()`, talk to your machine. Call `stop()` when
done.

```swift
@MainActor public final class VoiceChat {
    public init(config: VoiceChatConfig = VoiceChatConfig())
    public func start() async throws
    public func stop() async
    public func setSystemPrompt(_ prompt: String) async
    public func setVoicePreset(_ preset: String) async
    public func swapAvatarPortrait(url: URL) async throws
    public func playAvatarAudio(samples24k: [Float]) async
    public func previewVoice(_ preset: String) async
    public var orchestrator: VoiceChatOrchestrator? { get }
    public var bithuman: Bithuman? { get }
    public var initialIdleFrame: CGImage? { get }
    public var onBargeIn: (@Sendable () -> Void)?
    public var onCheckSpeechBuffer: (@Sendable () -> Bool)?
    public nonisolated let botSpeaking: ActivityFlag
    public nonisolated let llmActivity: ActivityFlag
    public nonisolated let swapActivity: ActivityFlag
}
```

## VoiceChatConfig / AvatarConfig / VoiceSelection

```swift
public struct VoiceChatConfig: Sendable {
    public var localeIdentifier: String = "en-US"
    public var systemPrompt: String? = nil
    public var voice: VoiceSelection = .preset("Aiden")
    public var avatar: AvatarConfig? = nil               // opt-in video mode
    public init()
}

public struct AvatarConfig: Sendable {
    public var modelPath: URL    // path to the universal weights artifact
    public var portraitPath: URL // JPG/PNG/HEIC
    public init(modelPath: URL, portraitPath: URL)
}

public enum VoiceSelection: Sendable {
    case preset(String)
    case clone(referenceAudio: URL, transcript: String)
}
```

## ExpressionWeights

```swift
public enum ExpressionWeights {
    public static var localURL: URL { get }
    public static func ensureAvailable(
        progress: (@Sendable (DownloadPhase) -> Void)? = nil
    ) async throws -> URL
}

public enum DownloadPhase: Sendable {
    case verifying
    case downloading(fraction: Double, bytes: Int64, total: Int64,
                     bytesPerSec: Double, etaSeconds: Double?)
    case verifyingDownloaded
    case ready
}
```

## AvatarCoordinator

```swift
@MainActor
public final class AvatarCoordinator: ObservableObject {
    public init(chat: VoiceChat)
    public func bindToOrchestrator()
    public weak var framePump: FramePump?

    // Reactive state — bind to SwiftUI views via @ObservedObject
    @Published public var orchestratorState: VoiceChatOrchestrator.State
    @Published public var swapState: SwapState                  // .idle / .encoding(label:)
    @Published public var isReady: Bool                         // first frame rendered
    @Published public var idlePrewarmReady: Bool                // warm-up complete
    @Published public var idlePrewarmProgress: Double           // [0, 1]
    @Published public var prewarmPortraitURL: URL?              // shown inside splash
    @Published public var muteAgentDuringPrewarm: Bool          // set on swap, cleared on completion
    @Published public var currentAgentCode: String?
    @Published public var currentVoicePreset: String
    @Published public var currentSystemPrompt: String

    // iOS-only sheet flags (toggled by show*Picker())
    @Published public var voicePickerPresented: Bool
    @Published public var promptEditorPresented: Bool
    @Published public var agentPickerPresented: Bool
    @Published public var portraitPickerPresented: Bool

    // Commands
    public func swapPortrait(url: URL)
    public func applyAgent(_ agent: Agent)         // face + voice + prompt
    public func setVoicePreset(_ preset: String)
    public func setSystemPrompt(_ prompt: String)
    public func previewVoice(_ preset: String)
    public func showVoicePicker()
    public func showAgentPicker()
    public func showPromptEditor()
    public func showPortraitPicker()
    public func resetIdlePrewarm()
    public func updateIdlePrewarm(count: Int, total: Int)
    public func markEngineReady()

    public var isSwapping: Bool { get }   // case .encoding active
}

public enum SwapState: Equatable, Sendable {
    case idle
    case encoding(label: String)
}
```

## FramePump + AvatarFrameSink

```swift
public final class FramePump: @unchecked Sendable {
    public init(bithuman: Bithuman, chat: VoiceChat,
                window: AvatarFrameSink, coordinator: AvatarCoordinator)
    public let buffer: FrameBuffer
    public func cancel()
    public func waitForProducerStop(timeoutMs: Int)
    public func resetIdleCache()
}

public protocol AvatarFrameSink: AnyObject {
    func render(_ frame: CGImage)
}

// Both NSView and UIView versions of AvatarRendererView conform.
public final class AvatarRendererView: PlatformView, AvatarFrameSink {
    public init(frame: CGRect, idleFrame: CGImage?, clipMode: ClipMode = .fill)
    public enum ClipMode { case fill, circle }
}
```

## SwiftUI components

```swift
// Main avatar tree (macOS only — iPad/iPhone apps build their own)
public struct AvatarRootView: View {
    public init(rendererView: AvatarRendererView, coordinator: AvatarCoordinator)
}

// Splash with optional progress arc + portrait underlay
public struct LoadingParticleField: View {
    public init(size: CGFloat, caption: String?,
                progress: Double? = nil, portraitURL: URL? = nil)
}

// First-launch download+warming UI
public struct BootSplashView: View {
    public init(progress: Double?, percentText: String?,
                detailText: String?, label: String?, portraitURL: URL?)
}

// Pickers (cross-platform)
public struct AgentPickerView: View
public struct VoicePickerView: View
public struct PromptEditorView: View

// Decorative
public struct StateRing: View
public struct StateLabel: View
public struct BithumanWordmark: View
public struct DownloadProgressView: View
public struct UnsupportedDeviceView: View
public enum BrandColors { public static let coral: Color /* … */ }
```

## Catalogs

```swift
public struct Agent: Sendable, Hashable {
    public let code: String           // e.g. "A29QAR4629" (Diego)
    public let name: String           // "Diego"
    public let category: String       // "Coach"
    public let voicePreset: String    // voice preset id
    public let systemPrompt: String
}

public enum AgentCatalog {
    public static let all: [Agent]            // 8 personas
    public static let defaultAgent: Agent     // Diego
    public static func thumbnailURL(for: Agent) -> URL?
}

public enum PortraitGallery {
    public static let presets: [(name: String, url: URL)]   // 5 portraits
}

public enum VoiceCatalog {
    public static let presetNames: [String]
    public static func displayName(for preset: String) -> String
}

public enum PromptTemplate {
    public static let starters: [(name: String, body: String)]  // 6 templates
}

public enum VoiceSelection {
    public static let presetNames: [String]   // voice presets
    public static func canonicalPreset(matching: String) -> String?  // case-insensitive
}
```

## Hardware check

```swift
public enum HardwareCheck {
    @MainActor
    public static func evaluate() -> DeviceCapability
}

public enum DeviceCapability: Equatable, Sendable {
    case supported
    case unsupported(reason: String)
}
```

## LLM helper

```swift
// Compose system prompt + TTS-friendly suffix. Internal callers
// already use this; exposed for embedders that want to preview
// the exact text the LLM sees.
public func composeLLMInstructions(_ prompt: String) -> String

public let defaultSystemPrompt: String
```

================================================================
SECTION 3 — WORKED EXAMPLES
================================================================

## Example 1 — Voice-only chat (no avatar), 30 lines

```swift
import SwiftUI
import bitHumanKit

@main
struct MyApp: App {
    @StateObject private var lifecycle = MyLifecycle()
    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ContentView(lifecycle: lifecycle).task {
                await lifecycle.start()
            }
        }
    }
}

@MainActor
final class MyLifecycle: ObservableObject {
    @Published var state = "idle"
    private var chat: VoiceChat?

    func start() async {
        var config = VoiceChatConfig()
        config.systemPrompt = "You are a friendly co-pilot. One sentence."
        config.voice = .preset("Aiden")
        let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
        do { try await chat.start(); self.chat = chat; state = "live" }
        catch { state = "error: \(error.localizedDescription)" }
    }
}

struct ContentView: View {
    @ObservedObject var lifecycle: MyLifecycle
    var body: some View {
        Text(lifecycle.state).font(.title)
    }
}
```

## Example 2 — Avatar chat with default agent, full Mac SwiftUI app

```swift
import SwiftUI
import bitHumanKit

@main
struct MyAvatarApp: App {
    @StateObject private var lifecycle = AvatarLifecycle()
    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            RootView(lifecycle: lifecycle).task { await lifecycle.start() }
        }
    }
}

@MainActor
final class AvatarLifecycle: ObservableObject {
    @Published var coordinator: AvatarCoordinator?
    @Published var renderer: AvatarRendererView?
    private var chat: VoiceChat?
    private var pump: FramePump?

    func start() async {
        do {
            let weights = try await ExpressionWeights.ensureAvailable()
            let agent = AgentCatalog.defaultAgent
            let portrait = AgentCatalog.thumbnailURL(for: agent)!

            var config = VoiceChatConfig()
            config.systemPrompt = agent.systemPrompt
            config.avatar = AvatarConfig(modelPath: weights, portraitPath: portrait)
            let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
            try await chat.start()
            await chat.setVoicePreset(agent.voicePreset)
            guard let bh = chat.bithuman else { return }

            let coord = AvatarCoordinator(chat: chat)
            coord.bindToOrchestrator()
            coord.currentAgentCode = agent.code
            coord.prewarmPortraitURL = portrait

            let renderer = AvatarRendererView(
                frame: .zero, idleFrame: chat.initialIdleFrame, clipMode: .circle)
            let pump = FramePump(
                bithuman: bh, chat: chat, window: renderer, coordinator: coord)
            coord.framePump = pump
            chat.onBargeIn = { [weak pump] in pump?.buffer.flushSpeech() }

            self.chat = chat
            self.pump = pump
            self.renderer = renderer
            self.coordinator = coord
        } catch {
            print("error: \(error)")
        }
    }
}

struct RootView: View {
    @ObservedObject var lifecycle: AvatarLifecycle
    var body: some View {
        if let coord = lifecycle.coordinator, let renderer = lifecycle.renderer {
            AvatarRootView(rendererView: renderer, coordinator: coord)
        } else {
            LoadingParticleField(size: 280, caption: "warming up…")
        }
    }
}
```

## Example 3 — Custom voice via cloning

```swift
var config = VoiceChatConfig()
config.voice = .clone(
    referenceAudio: Bundle.main.url(forResource: "myvoice", withExtension: "wav")!,
    transcript: "The transcript of myvoice.wav, 10–20 seconds of clean mono speech."
)
let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
try await chat.start()
// Reference audio is loaded once and used for every TTS call.
```

## Example 4 — Hot-swap agents from a SwiftUI button

```swift
Button("Try Diego") {
    let diego = AgentCatalog.all.first { $0.code == "A29QAR4629" }!
    coordinator.applyAgent(diego)
    // applyAgent: switches face, voice, and system prompt. Splash
    // auto-fires; muteAgentDuringPrewarm cuts the previous agent
    // until the new one is ready.
}
```

## Example 5 — User-supplied portrait via PhotosPicker (iOS)

```swift
import PhotosUI

@State private var pickedItem: PhotosPickerItem?

PhotosPicker(selection: $pickedItem, matching: .images) {
    Text("Change face")
}
.onChange(of: pickedItem) { _, item in
    Task {
        guard let data = try? await item?.loadTransferable(type: Data.self),
              let normalized = normalizeOrientation(data: data) else { return }
        let tmp = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
            .appendingPathComponent("portrait-\(UUID()).jpg")
        try? normalized.write(to: tmp)
        coordinator.swapPortrait(url: tmp)
    }
}
```

## Example 6 — Hardware-gated app launch (iOS)

```swift
@main
struct MyApp: App {
    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            switch HardwareCheck.evaluate() {
            case .supported:
                ContentView()
            case .unsupported(let reason):
                UnsupportedDeviceView(reason: reason)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

================================================================
SECTION 4 — ERROR HANDLING (typed)
================================================================

| Error type | Where it throws | How to handle |
|---|---|---|
| `Bithuman.Error.unsupportedHardware(reason:)` | Engine init (macOS) | Catch, show `UnsupportedDeviceView`. Pre-empt on iOS via `HardwareCheck.evaluate()`. |
| `WeightsError.checksumMismatch(expected:actual:)` | `ExpressionWeights.ensureAvailable` | Delete cached file at `ExpressionWeights.localURL`; rerun. |
| `WeightsError.network(underlying:)` | `ExpressionWeights.ensureAvailable` | Show retry; check connectivity; user can also pre-stage the weights into `localURL`. |
| Mic permission denied | `chat.start()` | Catch, show retry UI. iOS: bind to `AVAudioSession.requestRecordPermission`. macOS: System Settings → Privacy → Microphone. |
| Speech permission denied | `chat.start()` | Same shape; `SFSpeechRecognizer.requestAuthorization`. |
| iOS jetsam mid-conversation | OS-level | Add `increased-memory-limit` + `extended-virtual-addressing` entitlements. Apple approval 1–3 business days. |

================================================================
SECTION 5 — BUILDING + RELEASING
================================================================

## Local development
```sh
swift build                          # cross-platform
swift test                           # unit tests
./validate-ios.sh                    # iOS Simulator compile check
./build.sh Release                   # builds bithuman-cli (macOS exec)
./bithuman-cli video                 # smoke-test video mode
```

## Mac DMG
```sh
SIGNING_IDENTITY="Developer ID Application: ..." ./release.sh 0.1.0
./publish.sh 0.1.0                   # uploads to homebrew tap
```

## iPad/iPhone TestFlight
```sh
./Apps/BithumanPad/Scripts/build-ipad-app.sh 0.1.0
./Apps/BithumanPhone/Scripts/build-iphone-app.sh 0.1.0
```
First-time prereqs (Apple-side, manual): bundle ID registration,
`increased-memory-limit` + `extended-virtual-addressing` entitlement
approval, App Store Connect record, App Store Connect API key for
fully-automated upload.

## DocC docs
```sh
./scripts/build-docc.sh              # produces dist/docc/
# Push dist/docc/ contents to gh-pages branch for hosting.
```

================================================================
SECTION 6 — POINTERS
================================================================

- API reference: https://bithuman-product.github.io/bithuman-kit/

- AI-pair-programming guide: https://docs.bithuman.ai/agentic
- Reference apps: https://github.com/bithuman-product/bithuman-apps
- CLI tap: https://github.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bithuman
- Issues: https://github.com/bithuman-product/bithuman-kit/issues
- Email: hello@bithuman.ai

For the compact ingest, see `llms.txt`.
